data.title: Ooredoo Group's Syntys acquires Q Data facilities in Qatar, bringing 12.5MW hyperscale capacity to Free Zones DOHA.
data.description: Ooredoo Group and Syntys have revealed a recent agreement resulting in the acquisition of Q Data QFZ LLC by Syntys in Qatar. This deal will bring 12.5MW of hyperscale capacity to Syntys in Qatar Free Zones, signifying a significant development in the region's data center capabilities. The facilities acquired by Syntys are known for serving prominent cloud and artificial intelligence services in Qatar. This partnership showcases a strategic move towards boosting Syntys' presence in the market and enhancing its capabilities within the hyperscale data center sector.

data.title: Mercantile Bank raises quarterly cash dividend to $0.39, offering 3.1% annual yield on common stock.
data.description: The Board of Mercantile Bank Corporation (NASDAQ: MBWM) has approved a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.39 per common stock, equating to an annual yield of around 3.1%. The announcement was made on January 20, 2026, in Grand Rapids, Michigan.

data.title: FITUR 2026 unites global tourism, with 160+ countries and 10,000 companies. Mexico joins as Partner Country, with 967 main exhibitors and 11% more international participation."urls".
data.description: In the upcoming FITUR 2026 event, Mexico will participate as the Partner Country alongside 967 primary exhibitors, showcasing an 11% increase in international participants. The event will feature the Knowledge Hub, FITUR Experience, and the inaugural International Summit on Communication and Tourism, highlighting major innovations in the industry.

data.title: Podcast "On the School Bus with Dr. Spates" submitted for Webby Awards in 3 categories. LA, Jan 20, 2026.
data.description: In Los Angeles on January 20, 2026, the podcast "On the School Bus with Dr. Spates" has been entered for review in the prestigious Webby Awards. The podcast, which highlights successful practices in American schools, is being considered in three categories: Education, Best...

data.title: Bybit, 2nd largest cryptocurrency exchange, discusses 2026 market trends in livestream.
data.description: In Dubai, the United Arab Emirates on January 20, 2026, Bybit, the second-largest cryptocurrency exchange globally based on trading volume, organized a livestream event called "Kick off 2026 with Bybit Alpha & Bybit xStocks." The session concentrated on the impact of macroeconomic factors, corporate earnings, and geopolitical events.

data.title: Hong Kong group cautions on crypto licensing, fearing shutdowns. New licenses expand oversight on virtual assets.
data.description: As Hong Kong considers new licenses for virtual asset advisory and management, warnings about the need for expanded oversight have emerged. This move could potentially impact a range of entities beyond just cryptocurrency trading platforms.

data.title: UBS names Google as quantum computing pioneer, predicts quantum advantage by 2030s. Quantum market seen as fragmented with potential in molecular simulation, optimization, AI, and cryptography. Qubits can be made in various ways with superconducting and trapped-ion qubits leading the field.
data.description: UBS, a leading financial services company, has highlighted several top quantum computing stocks leading the industry and emphasizing their "extraordinary potential." The analysts, headed by Madeleine Jenkins, have pointed out that quantum computing is currently a fragmented and immature market, showcasing significant promise in areas like molecular simulation, optimization, AI, and cryptography. Recent advancements in the quantum space have been labeled as "meaningful breakthroughs," although progress has been slow. According to UBS, the era of quantum advantage, where quantum computing significantly surpasses classical systems, may emerge as early as the 2030s. The report underlines that qubits, which are the fundamental units of quantum computing, can be created in various ways, with superconducting and trapped-ion qubits leading the field. This information is available for further reading on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Envision Energy partners with SKI to expand energy storage in Central Asia; Joint Development Agreement for BESS production.
data.description: In Abu Dhabi, UAE on January 20, 2026, Envision Energy, a renowned company in green technology, revealed a significant partnership with Samruk-Kazyna Invest (SKI), the investment branch of Kazakhstan's sovereign wealth fund. This collaboration involves a Joint Development Agreement aiming to establish localized production facilities for Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) at Envision Global. This move signifies a notable step towards sustainable energy practices and fostering cooperation between the two entities.
